core_payload.content:
# Tracker Orchestration Signals

## 1. Role
Tracker Orchestration Signals owns the runtime-facing risk feeds that move execution truth into action lanes. It packages staleness, drift, blockage, and overload as input that swarms, jobs, and orchestration runtimes can interpret without guessing at the state of work.

## 2. Orchestration Signals Surface
This sub-hub groups `n-infinity`, `capsule-graph-maintenance`, `capsule-job`, `symphony`, and `symphony-run-attempt`. Together they define how Tracker telemetry becomes risk input for swarm intervention, queue discipline, issue execution, and maintenance routing.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own the source work objects, reminder timing, or long-horizon analytics. Those responsibilities belong to `tracked-objects`, `schedule-pressure`, and `historical-intelligence`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Tracker Orchestration Signals groups the runtime-facing risk feeds of the tracker system: N-Infinity, Capsule Graph Maintenance, Capsule Job, Symphony, and Symphony Run Attempt. It keeps staleness, blockage, drift, and overload signals together so swarms and orchestration runtimes can act on execution truth without guessing at work state. This boundary matters because the bridge from tracking into live intervention should stay explicit rather than diffusing across unrelated runtime domains. It gives N1Hub one named seam where telemetry becomes queue pressure, swarm action, and maintenance escalation.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Tracker decomposition to isolate runtime-facing risk signals for swarms, jobs, and orchestration from local reminder and analytics surfaces.
